title: [Feature]: Evaluate WebSocket connection support
problem: Some NATS deployments expose WebSocket connectivity for constrained network environments. nats-sinks currently documents standard NATS connection modes but does not certify WebSocket connections. Supporting this mode requires understanding client behavior, TLS expectations, proxy behavior, and production suitability.
proposal: Evaluate WebSocket connection support through nats-py capabilities, configuration fields, TLS settings, and integration tests. If supported, document the mode clearly and add tests proving that connection setup works without changing the runner’s delivery semantics.
users: Operators in environments where direct NATS TCP connectivity is unavailable, maintainers tracking NATS client compatibility, and security reviewers assessing proxy and TLS behavior.
delivery_semantics: Connection transport must not change commit-then-ACK behavior. Reconnect, timeout, and shutdown behavior must remain consistent with existing connection handling.
security: WebSocket transport must validate TLS by default, avoid insecure proxy assumptions, and redact connection configuration. Documentation must avoid real network details and must clearly state production caveats.
acceptance: WebSocket support is evaluated and documented.
Configuration validation covers supported transport settings.
Tests or manual validation guidance are added.
Reconnect behavior is described for this transport.
tests: Add unit tests for configuration and option construction. Add optional integration tests only when local test infrastructure exists. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/configuration.md, docs/security.md, docs/operations.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after released implementation or documented no-go decision. Include release tag or decision note, tests, and limitations.
